Date issued | Description of change in newsletter
2011-12-13  *(corrected  2011-12-15)* | Name precision of the administrative division, Alphabetical re-ordering and source list update.

is the entry for [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") in [ISO 3166-2](/wiki/ISO_3166-2 "ISO 3166-2"), part of the [ISO 3166](/wiki/ISO_3166 "ISO 3166") [standard](/wiki/Standardization "Standardization") published by the [International Organization for Standardization](/wiki/International_Organization_for_Standardization "International Organization for Standardization") (ISO), which defines [codes](/wiki/Code "Code") for the names of the principal [subdivisions](/wiki/Country_subdivision "Country subdivision") (e.g., [provinces](/wiki/Province "Province") or [states](/wiki/State_(administrative_division) "State (administrative division)")) of all [countries](/wiki/Country "Country") coded in [ISO 3166-1](/wiki/ISO_3166-1 "ISO 3166-1").
For reference only, English name not included in the ISO 3166-2 standard.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Entry for Sweden in ISO 3166-2

| [Administrative divisions of Sweden](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Sweden "Administrative divisions of Sweden") |
| --- |
| [Counties of Sweden](/wiki/Counties_of_Sweden "Counties of Sweden") |
| * [Administrative boards](/wiki/County_administrative_boards_of_Sweden "County administrative boards of Sweden") * [Regions](/wiki/Regions_of_Sweden "Regions of Sweden") |
| [Municipalities of Sweden](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Sweden "Municipalities of Sweden") |
| [List of municipalities](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_of_Sweden "List of municipalities of Sweden") |
| Other divisions |
| * Historical divisions ([Lands](/wiki/Lands_of_Sweden "Lands of Sweden") and [Provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Sweden "Provinces of Sweden")) * [Statistical regions](/wiki/NUTS_statistical_regions_of_Sweden "NUTS statistical regions of Sweden") * ISO codes for subdivisions   --- |
| flag [Sweden portal](/wiki/Portal:Sweden "Portal:Sweden") |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:Subdivisions_of_Sweden "Template:Subdivisions of Sweden") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Subdivisions_of_Sweden "Template talk:Subdivisions of Sweden") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Subdivisions_of_Sweden "Special:EditPage/Template:Subdivisions of Sweden") |

**ISO 3166-2:SE** is the entry for [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") in [ISO 3166-2](/wiki/ISO_3166-2 "ISO 3166-2"), part of the [ISO 3166](/wiki/ISO_3166 "ISO 3166") [standard](/wiki/Standardization "Standardization") published by the [International Organization for Standardization](/wiki/International_Organization_for_Standardization "International Organization for Standardization") (ISO), which defines [codes](/wiki/Code "Code") for the names of the principal [subdivisions](/wiki/Country_subdivision "Country subdivision") (e.g., [provinces](/wiki/Province "Province") or [states](/wiki/State_(administrative_division) "State (administrative division)")) of all [countries](/wiki/Country "Country") coded in [ISO 3166-1](/wiki/ISO_3166-1 "ISO 3166-1").

Currently for Sweden, ISO 3166-2 codes are defined for [21 counties](/wiki/Counties_of_Sweden "Counties of Sweden").

Each code consists of two parts, separated by a hyphen. The first part is SE, the [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2 "ISO 3166-1 alpha-2") code of Sweden. The second part is one or two letters, which is the alphabetic code ([Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_language "Swedish language"): *länsbokstav*) of the county, and was used in [vehicle registration plates](/wiki/Vehicle_registration_plates_of_Sweden "Vehicle registration plates of Sweden") until 1973.

## Current codes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=ISO_3166-2:SE&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Current codes")]

Subdivision names are listed as in the ISO 3166-2 standard published by the ISO 3166 Maintenance Agency (ISO 3166/MA).

In ISO 3166-2, the numeric code (*länskod*) of each county is shown for information purposes in square brackets after the name of the county. However, these alternative code elements are not official ISO 3166-2 codes, since this part of ISO 3166 does not allow for duplicate coding of identical subdivisions.

Subdivision names are sorted in [Swedish alphabetical](/wiki/Swedish_alphabet "Swedish alphabet") order: a–z, å, ä, ö.

*Click on the button in the header to sort each column.*
